ANSWER TO PETITION FOR WRIT OF MANDATE/ PROHIBITION/ CERTIFICATION FILED BY RESPONDENT CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO
MINI-MINUTES FOR NOV-04-2009 9:30 AM FOR DEPT 302

LAW AND MOTION 302, PETITIONER TENDERLION HOUSING CLINIC MOTION FOR ISSUANCE OF PEREMPTORY WRIT OF MANDATE. ARGUED AND THE COURT ADOPTED ITS TENTATIVE RULING AS FOLLOWS: MOTION DENIED. NO SHOWING THAT THE CITY'S ACTION IN AWARDING THE GRANT CONTRACT TO MARY ELIZABETH INN (MEI) WAS ARBITRARY OR CAPRICIOUS. SUBSTANTIAL EVIDENCE SUPPORT'S THE CITY'S DECISION. MEI'S LEASE CONSTITUTES A MASTER LEASE, AND MEI HAS BEEN SUBLEASING THE BUILDING FOR MORE THAN THREE YEARS. ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. JUDGE: CHARLOTTE WALTER WOOLARD, REPORTER: KENT GUBBINE, CSR #5797

calendar.matters:
Notice Of Motion And Otion For Issuance Of Peremptory Writ Of Mandate
Off Calendar Nov-04-2009 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: PETITIONER TENDERLION HOUSING CLINIC Motion for Issuance of Peremptory Writ of Mandate. Appearances: Erin Bernstein, Deputy City Attorney, CCSF,415) 554-3975 appearing for Defendant/Respondent CCSF; Matt McFarland, Staff Atty. Tenderloin Housing Clinic (415) 771-9850, appearing for Petitioner and moving party. Argued and the Court adopted its tentative ruling as follows: Motion denied. No showing that the City's action in awarding the grant contract to Mary Elizabeth Inn (MEI) was arbitrary or capricious. Substantial evidence support's the City's decision. MEI's lease constitutes a master lease, and MEI has been subleasing the building for more than three years. Order signed in open Court.(302/CWW)Judge: Charlotte Walter Woolard; Court Reporter: Kent Gubbine CSR 5797

Notice Of Motion And Otion For Issuance Of Peremptory Writ Of Mandate

The Court adopted and amended its tentative ruling as follows: Hearing Continued to November 4, 2009 per agreement of parties.
